nodes.label: AI as writing and formatting assistant for menus
nodes.label_negative: AI involvement in creative menu planning
nodes.grounding_span: I always make it very clear to the model that I do not want any advice or suggestions in relation to the menu items or food, the work is strictly on the writing and formatting of the menu itself.

nodes.label: AI as writing assistant for drafting
nodes.label_negative: AI as one-shot perfect output generator
nodes.grounding_span: I usually try to get a few options for everything, and pick the parts I like. Then I make a draft, and get the model to refine it and provide opinions.

nodes.label: AI's ability to produce authentic voice
nodes.label_negative: AI sanitized or overly kind tone
nodes.grounding_span: Sometimes, they models will make me sound TOO kind, which is probably appropriate most of the time, but it doesn't match my voice.

nodes.label: AI as time-saving tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement for creative work
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI as a shortcut to get the non-food related stuff done, but I still focus on making sure it's on-brand

nodes.label: AI as recipe suggestion tool for specialized dietary needs
nodes.label_negative: AI errors in recipe math and precision
nodes.grounding_span: I've learned the hard way that I need to double check every recipe provided by the model or just do it myself beforehand.

nodes.label: AI-generated content quality and validity
nodes.label_negative: AI-generated content as lazy and impersonal
nodes.grounding_span: When I see that something is clearly just copy pasted from an AI model I get put off. To me, personality shows care and pride.

nodes.label: Creative autonomy
nodes.grounding_span: I look cooking, and I like creating dishes. If I was just plugging requests in to a machine and following recipes, I'd probably do something else.

nodes.label: Client trust and credibility
nodes.grounding_span: I want the students to feel like it's coming from me personally.

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: Saved me many hours of work.

nodes.label: Professional reputation and credibility
nodes.grounding_span: If you aren't putting any effort in to your menus or website, can I trust you to care about the food you put out?

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for drafting
nodes.grounding_span: I actually really appreciate the models for that - I can just write what I feel and let them clean it up afterwards.

nodes.label: Distrust of AI for precise recipe formulation
nodes.grounding_span: I always get frustrated when making dough recipe books.

nodes.label: Frustration with AI formatting
nodes.grounding_span: I felt very satisfied when I used AI to create a 12 tab Google Doc that I give to guests on a QR code.

nodes.label: Distrust of AI-generated menus as lazy
nodes.grounding_span: When I see that something is clearly just copy pasted from an AI model I get put off.

nodes.label: Commitment to collaborative creative process
nodes.grounding_span: Even if I had full trust in the model, I think I would need to keep things collaborative as a creative output.

nodes.label: Iterative refinement with limited back-and-forth
nodes.grounding_span: I'll usually evaluate it side by side.

nodes.label: Domain-based boundary setting — separates creative from non-creative tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I always make it very clear to the model that I do not want any advice or suggestions in relation to the menu items or food
